name: migration_patterns_v1
description: Database migration patterns for adapterOS SQLite schema management. Covers schema migrations (CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX), data migrations (backfill, seed, transform), migration safety (safe vs dangerous operations, blue-green upgrades), rollback procedures (backup/restore, point-in-time recovery, partial failure handling), and BLAKE3 signature verification (tamper detection, chain of trust, CI enforcement). 1500 examples grounded in the actual migrations/ directory (325 .sql files, WAL mode, forward-only policy, sqlx runtime).

entries.notes: 1500 migration examples: schema migrations (300), data migrations (300), migration safety (300), rollback procedures (300), signature verification (300)

provenance.review_notes: Database migration operations dataset covering the full lifecycle of adapterOS SQLite migrations. Forward-only migration policy with BLAKE3 integrity verification, Ed25519 signatures, WAL mode for concurrent reads, and backup-based rollback. Grounded in actual migration files from 0001_init.sql through 0292_backend_compile_flags.sql.

evaluation_gates: Schema migrations use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN always uses DEFAULT or allows NULL
Data migrations use UPDATE with WHERE guards against re-application
Trigger-based constraint validation uses RAISE(ABORT) pattern
Rollback always involves backup restore, never manual DDL reversal
BLAKE3 hashes computed over raw .sql file bytes, not parsed SQL
Ed25519 signatures cover the BLAKE3 hash, not the file directly
WAL mode enables concurrent reads during migration execution
Migration lock prevents concurrent migration attempts at startup
Forward-only policy: no DOWN migrations, no DROP TABLE in production
intent: Train the model to reason about adapterOS database migration operations: when and how to write schema changes, how to safely transform data, what operations are dangerous in production, how to recover from failed migrations, and how the BLAKE3/Ed25519 signature chain ensures migration integrity from developer to production.
